name: Catfish Stew
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy-bottomed soup pot, sauté the bacon until it begins to brown.
2. Add the onions and diced green and red peppers; cook for about 5 minutes longer.
3. Stir in the diced tomatoes and cook for another 5 minutes.
4. Add the okra and fish stock; bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer.
5. Cook until the soup begins to thicken (tomato paste can be added at this point if desired).
6. Add the cubed catfish and simmer until the fish is cooked through.
7. Season with salt and pepper.
8. Pass over crushed red pepper flakes.
Note: To spice up the soup, add tabasco or green/red pepper. Crab, shrimp, oysters, or chicken can also be added. Caught a few catfish recently that were too small to keep; this recipe came to mind. It's great when made spicy and served with cold beer.
